restricted.name: Restricted
restricted.subtitle: Just Bash, no sandbox
restricted.description: Minimal execution environment. No sandbox runtime, no MCP support.

sandbox.name: Sandbox
sandbox.subtitle: Configurable isolation
sandbox.description: Sandbox runtime with configurable filesystem, network, and command restrictions.

unrestricted.name: Unrestricted
unrestricted.subtitle: Full system access
unrestricted.description: No sandbox restrictions. All commands run with full system permissions.

config.editingDefault: Editing default values. Click Save to create a new named configuration.

modal.unrestrictedBody: This removes all sandbox protection. AI agents will have full access to your system, including:
modal.unrestrictedReadWrite: Read/write any file on your computer
modal.unrestrictedFooter: Only use this for local development in trusted environments.
modal.sandboxUnavailableBody: Sandbox runtime prerequisites are not met. The following issues were found:
modal.sandboxUnavailableFooter: Enabling sandbox mode anyway will cause runtime degradation to restricted mode.
modal.deleteBody: This configuration will be permanently deleted. The project will revert to system defaults.

filesystem.allowWriteHelper: Paths where agents can write files. Default: project workspace directory.
filesystem.denyReadHelper: Sensitive files/folders to block from reading.
filesystem.denyWriteHelper: Paths where writing is blocked. Takes precedence over allow write.

network.allowedDomainsHelper: Domains agents can access. Exact: example.com — Wildcard: *.example.com (2+ parts after *.)
network.deniedDomainsHelper: Domains to block. Same format as allowed domains.

mcp.warning: (⚠ Disabling this may allow third-party MCP servers to access or modify files on your computer)
mcp.description: When enabled, MCP server commands will be wrapped with sandbox runtime (srt). This applies the same filesystem, network, and command restrictions to MCP servers.

windows.message: Sandbox runtime is not available on Windows. Only command blocking is supported.

validation.removeProtocol: Remove protocol, path, or port — just the domain
validation.validDomain: Must be a valid domain with at least one dot (e.g., example.com)
